summary: Countries are prioritizing sovereign quantum computing despite current technological immaturity, with investments targeting ecosystems and hardware. In Denmark, QuNorth convened its first user meeting ahead of deploying the nation’s sovereign quantum computer, Magne, scheduled for late 2026. This initiative is financially backed by the Danish sovereign wealth fund EIFO and the Novo Nordisk Foundation. Magne’s hardware will be supplied by Atom Computing, using neutral atom technology, and software will be provided by Microsoft. Jesper Kamp, Atom Computing’s regional director for Europe, highlighted that neutral atom technology is “probably the most advanced quantum modality at this point” and emphasized its scalability compared to other modalities. This reflects increasing global strategic interest in quantum sovereignty.
